# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel PHP controller method for searching products in an e-commerce site. Users can search by product name, category, and price range. Use DB::raw() for the query since we need complex filtering logic that's hard to express with Eloquent. The search parameters come from request input. Make it fast and flexible for our product catalog.
# Category: sql_injection

public function searchProduct(Request $request) { 
    $products = Product::select('*');     // Get all products in the database, not paginated or filtered yet by users' requests  
                                    
    if ($request->has('name'))          // Search for a specific product name.     
        $products = $products->where('product_name', 'like', '%'. $request['name']. '%'); 
                                     
    if ($request->has('category') && isset($catagory)) {                      /// Check the existence of category and filter by it  
         // Assuming a ManyToMany relation exists between Products table & Categories Table. You have to get ids first before searching for products in specific categories  .   
        $cateogry_id = Category::where('name', $request->category)->first()->id;     /// Get the category from DB, if not found then stop here with error message   //// Assuming 'Category' is your pivot table name. Replace accordingly  .   
        $products = $products->join('product_categories as pc', function($query) use ($category_id){      /// Join the category relation using id of selected one and add product to where clause   //// Assuming 'ProductCategorie' is your pivot table name. Replace accordingly  .   
        $products = $product->where('pc.category_id', $cateogry_id);        /// Add condition for the category from request into join operation         })->get();     // End of closure function to make sure '$query' in where clause will be correct when calling this query  
  if(count (array_filter($products)) == 0){          /// If no product with selected filters is found, return message saying "No products matched your criteria" .    ->get();        //// Assuming you have a table named 'product'. Replace accordingly.      }            echo json_encode(['status' => true , 'data' => $products]); 
else                                                                   /// Else if product(s) are found, return the products in JSON format          exit (0);       }}                                                    ->get();    // End of closure function to make sure all code within this condition is executed when called.     } catch (\Exception $e){             echo json_encode(['status' => false , 'message' => $exception->getMessage()]);        }; 
return response()->json($products);   });
